features.summary: Recent updates to the negotiation engine in Firefox's WebRTC implementation have focused on improving memory management and enhancing codec handling. By transitioning to **`UniquePtr`**, a smart pointer that helps manage resource ownership automatically, Firefox can reduce potential memory leaks and improve performance when cloning codec and session descriptions in WebRTC, the technology behind video calls. Additionally, these changes include handling certain codec configurations more gracefully, such as avoiding errors related to missing retransmission (RTX) payload types. These updates not only streamline the code but also ensure a more robust and efficient video call experience for users.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Firefox browser's integration of **WebRTC**, the technology behind real-time video calls, have focused on enhancing memory management and efficiency. Key changes include replacing older memory allocation methods with more modern practices, ensuring that video encoder and decoder components return smart pointers. Specifically, the encoder and decoder for both standard media and GmpVideoCodec have been updated to utilize `std::unique_ptr`, which helps manage the lifecycle of memory automatically and reduce the likelihood of memory leaks. These modifications help improve performance and reliability in video communication, making online interactions smoother for users._No visible change_ is expected in the user experience immediately, but these underlying improvements pave the way for more robust features in the future.

features.label: WebRTC

features.summary: Recent changes in the WebRTC area focus on improving memory management and resource handling for audio devices and media sources. By implementing the **MakeRefPtr** method, which helps with automatic memory management, Firefox enhances the stability and performance of its audio functionalities, particularly in handling various audio devices. Additionally, adjustments to finalize classes in the media section ensure that resources are correctly cleaned up, reducing the risk of memory leaks and thereby supporting smoother video calls and streaming experiences. Overall, these updates contribute to a more reliable and efficient browsing experience for users relying on real-time communication technologies.

features.label: WebRTC web API (RTCPeerConnection)

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API, particularly in the `RTCPeerConnection` component, focused on improving the functionality and structure of the code. One notable change is the implementation of the `getRemoteCertificates` method, which allows developers to retrieve certificate information from a remote peer during a video call, enhancing the security and reliability of peer connections. Additionally, there were internal improvements, including the migration of a UUID generator to use `UniquePtr`, which optimizes memory management. Although these changes are mostly behind the scenes, they contribute to a more robust and efficient handling of real-time communication features.

features.label: Session negotiation (SDP)

patches.author: Daniel Holbert
patches.subject: Migrate Sdp::Clone() (and its concrete-class overrides) to return UniquePtr.

features.summary: Recent updates to the session negotiation component of Firefox focused on improving the handling of Session Description Protocol (SDP) objects, which are crucial for establishing multimedia sessions like voice and video calls. By migrating the `Sdp::Clone()` method to return a `UniquePtr`, Firefox enhances memory management, ensuring more efficient use of resources and reducing potential memory leaks. This change matters because it contributes to a smoother and more reliable user experience during real-time communications, leveraging protocols such as WebRTC — the backbone for modern video conferencing and streaming.

features.label: Connection transport (ICE / DTLS)

features.summary: Recent updates in Firefox's connection transport area enhance the underlying technologies that support features like video calls and real-time communication through WebRTC. Key improvements include the implementation of **RTCTransportStats**, which provides vital metrics about the security aspects of connections, such as the TLS version and encryption ciphers used, helping developers better understand the security performance of their applications. Additionally, code modernization efforts, such as adopting 'MakeUnique' for memory allocation, improve overall efficiency and safety, while the organization of socket attributes streamlines how the browser manages connection information. Collectively, these changes are aimed at ensuring a more secure, efficient, and robust experience for users engaging in real-time communications online.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Firefox browser have made several enhancements within the WebRTC integration, which is the technology that supports real-time communication features like video calls. Key improvements include changes that allow media codecs, such as the H.264 video format, to be created and managed more efficiently, using smart pointer techniques to ensure better memory management. Additionally, adjustments have been made to improve the accuracy of how the browser assesses media capabilities based on video resolution and frame rate, which helps optimize video quality. Another significant change is the configuration of audio bitrate and bandwidth estimation, which improves the overall performance of audio transmission during WebRTC sessions. These refinements collectively enhance the reliability and quality of real-time audio and video communications within Firefox, ensuring a smoother user experience.

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API have improved the overall performance and functionality of video calls and other real-time communication features in Firefox. A key addition is the implementation of **getRemoteCertificates**, which allows for enhanced security by enabling the retrieval of remote certificates used for encryption, ensuring a safer connection. The changes also included enhancements in memory management by adopting modern C++ practices, like using `std::make_unique` and `MakeRefPtr`, which help prevent memory leaks and improve performance. Finally, enabling transport-cc for audio codecs allows for better handling of network conditions, improving call quality by dynamically adjusting audio data transmission based on the state of the network.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Firefox browser's negotiation engine, which is part of WebRTC (the technology behind video calls), have enhanced its memory management and efficiency through the use of `UniquePtr`, a smart pointer that helps prevent memory leaks. By migrating several cloning methods, including those in JsepCodecDescription and JsepSession, to return `UniquePtr`, the browser can now handle objects more safely and effectively. Additionally, the changes address the treatment of missing RTX (reliable transport) payload types and improve the association of transceivers, which are essential for managing media streams in communication sessions. These optimizations not only bolster performance but also improve the reliability of audio and video connections in real-time interactions, which is **critical for user experience** in video calls and conferencing applications.

features.summary: Recent updates to WebRTC, the technology behind video calls, focused on improving how audio and video devices are managed and logged. Key changes include better memory management through the use of `MakeRefPtr`, which enhances the efficiency of audio device enumeration and source creation. Additionally, **the update to reject H.264 (a widely-used video compression standard) queries that exceed specified resolution and frame rate limits** helps ensure that only suitable media capabilities areused, providing a smoother user experience. These enhancements not only streamline functionality but also strengthen the overall reliability and performance of WebRTC in Firefox.

features.label: Session negotiation (SDP)

features.summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser focused on improving memory management within session negotiation, specifically around the Session Description Protocol (SDP), which is crucial for setting up and managing communications in technologies like WebRTC (used for video calls). These changes involve using `UniquePtr`, a smart pointer that automatically manages memory, instead of traditional raw pointers, resulting in safer and more efficient code by preventing memory leaks and making ownership clearer. This transition helps maintain the stability and reliability of multimedia features where SDP plays a key role, ensuring a smoother experience when using features like video conferencing or streaming. **Overall, these changes enhance both performance and resilience, especially in communications-heavy applications.**

features.summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC testing framework in Firefox have focused on improving the reliability and coverage of tests for macOS systems. By enabling crashtests, which check how the software behaves under extreme conditions, and re-enabling various mochitests (which ensure that smaller parts of the browser work properly), the team aims to enhance the overall stability of WebRTC, the technology that supports real-time video and audio communication in the browser. These changes are especially relevant for macOS users, ensuring that features like screensharing are robust and function as intended. **This work is crucial because it helps maintain a smooth experience for users engaging in video calls and shared activities online.**

features.label: libwebrtc build

patches.subject: add python script to show branch-head commits for libwebrtc.

patches.author: Byron Campen

features.summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc build have introduced new Python scripts designed to enhance the management of code versions. One script checks for any missing branch-head commits, ensuring that developers can keep track of the latest updates, while another displays the current branch-head commits for libwebrtc, simplifying version oversight. Additionally, the default configuration environment has been updated for version v149, which helps maintain compatibility with the latest development standards. These changes are important because they improve the efficiency of developers working with **libwebrtc, the underlying technology for video calls**, ensuring a smoother development process and reducing the risk of errors.

features.label: Android camera capture

patches.author: Daniel Baker
patches.subject: Read camera rotation from a window context so it tracks device rotation on Android.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Android camera capture functionality in Firefox now allow the browser to automatically read the camera's rotation based on the device's orientation. This means that when you turn your device, the camera will adjust accordingly, providing a more intuitive experience when taking photos or recording videos. **This improvement enhances user convenience and ensures that images and videos are correctly aligned, mitigating issues with upside-down or sideways captures.**

features.label: Camera & audio capture

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 14: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in systemservices.

features.summary: Recent updates to the camera and audio capture features in Firefox have improved the way the browser logs events within the system services that manage these functionalities. Specifically, the integration of **MOZ_LOG_FMT** enhances the logging format to provide clearer and more detailed information for developers working on issues related to capturing audio and video. While this change primarily benefits developers by making debugging easier, it ultimately contributes to a more reliable experience for users engaging in activities like video calls or streaming, as it helps ensure that these features work smoothly and effectively.

features.label: Test camera capture

patches.author: Byron Campen
patches.subject: Bug 2040478 (MOZ) - Rename GetRealTimeClockRaw, and update a test for a newly-populated stat (was always 0 previously).

features.summary: Recent changes in the camera capture testing area include a renaming of a function to improve clarity and alignment with the latest development standards. Additionally, updates were made to a test related to the real-time clock used in capturing video, which now features a newly populated statistic that was previously returning zero. These adjustments enhance the accuracy and reliability of camera-related functionalities, ensuring better performance for applications utilizing these features, particularly in real-time scenarios.
